title: kaukenia littoralis
intro: Kaukenia littoralis is a plant species endemic to New Zealand, commonly known as the kawakawa tree, which has been used by the indigenous Māori people for its medicinal and spiritual significance. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in alkaloids, including caffeine and other stimulants, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The medicinal properties of Kaukenia littoralis include its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots are often infused in hot water to make a tea, or used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and infusions, to harness their medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
